title: daphne kiusiana var. atrocaulis
intro: Daphne kiusiana var. atrocaulis, also known as Korean daphne or purple daphne, is a shrub-like plant native to the Korean Peninsula, characterized by its purple flowers and dark green leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine and is believed to possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Daphne kiusiana var. atrocaulis include its potential to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and fever, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.
